wire_delay_model.links_note: 8x8 grid on 20mm x 20mm die, 2.5 mm tile pitch. 22nm delta=90.0 ps/mm, clock 3 GHz (period 333.33 ps). cardinal link 2.5 mm = 1 cycle(s); diagonal link 3.536 mm = 1 cycle(s). Generator: /tmp/hexa-arch-rfc001-measurement/runs/gen_topologies_v2.py.

router_cost.port_cost_basis: d6/d4 = 1.516x -- comb SKY130 yosys+abc measured (d4=61763 um^2, d6=93608 um^2), comb HANDOFF_TO_HEXA_ARCH.md §4

verdict.rationale: single-topology record; F1/F2 verdict requires the pair-verdict aggregator over this + a degree-paired mate at identical (traffic, node, clock, wire_delay_model)

wire_delay_source.node_basis: SMART 22nm TSMC GP, characterization Table 2; reaffirmed OpenSMART ISPASS 2017 III

leighton_source.edition: Leighton 1984 + Leighton 1992 1.4; Dally & Towles PPIN 2004 3.2-3.3 mesh closed forms

scope_caveats: single (3 GHz, 22nm, d6_hex placement) point under tornado -- one cell of the rfc_001 9 sweep, not a standalone regime claim
router_cost.port_*_norm is an analytic degree-scaling estimate (T1A 2 / Dally-Towles port-count), NOT measured silicon area
routing = min (DOR-class shortest-path); adaptive routing may shift saturation throughput
axial-hex-on-square-grid has hop-diameter 14 = the d=4 mesh diameter (no NW diagonal); the degree-6 advantage is avg-hops (4.54 vs 5.33) and bisection (15 vs 8), NOT diameter
